notes: Lesson 25 Part 1 (act 147) 'Consistent Hashing — a ring so adding a server moves few keys', 122.7s, 6 cues. Uses sysdesign.py hash_ring/ring_marker/ring_arc/owner_of. Verified: modulo reshuffle (5/6 keys move on N=4->5), ring first-clockwise ownership, add-server steals ≈1/N, virtual nodes smooth uneven arcs, binary-search O(log n) lookup, remove-server spills one arc ≈O(K/N). MathTex bounds legible. 2 -ql review iterations (round-1 caught to_edge bottom-buff safe-area overflow, right-panel fit_label width overflow, recolor_cell .box attr bug — all fixed); READ start/mid/end each cue from muxed mp4, all pass.

frames.notes: [start] server=hash(key) mod N table: keys k1..k6, N=4 row (1,2,3,0,3,0) blue, N=5 row (2,2,4,4,1,3) rose; 5 of 6 keys jump servers highlighted, 'cache-miss storm' note. Modulo math correct (17/22/39/44/51/68 mod 4 and 5).

[mid] server=hash(key) mod N table: keys k1..k6, N=4 row (1,2,3,0,3,0) blue, N=5 row (2,2,4,4,1,3) rose; 5 of 6 keys jump servers highlighted, 'cache-miss storm' note. Modulo math correct (17/22/39/44/51/68 mod 4 and 5).

[end] server=hash(key) mod N table: keys k1..k6, N=4 row (1,2,3,0,3,0) blue, N=5 row (2,2,4,4,1,3) rose; 5 of 6 keys jump servers highlighted, 'cache-miss storm' note. Modulo math correct (17/22/39/44/51/68 mod 4 and 5).

[start] hash ring with 3 servers S0@40(blue)/S1@160(violet)/S2@280(amber) and 3 keys k1@90/k2@210/k3@330; ownership arcs walk clockwise: k1->S1 violet, k2->S2 amber, k3->S0 blue (wrap); legend server/key; note 'first server clockwise owns it'. Arc directions correct.

[mid] hash ring with 3 servers S0@40(blue)/S1@160(violet)/S2@280(amber) and 3 keys k1@90/k2@210/k3@330; ownership arcs walk clockwise: k1->S1 violet, k2->S2 amber, k3->S0 blue (wrap); legend server/key; note 'first server clockwise owns it'. Arc directions correct.

[end] hash ring with 3 servers S0@40(blue)/S1@160(violet)/S2@280(amber) and 3 keys k1@90/k2@210/k3@330; ownership arcs walk clockwise: k1->S1 violet, k2->S2 amber, k3->S0 blue (wrap); legend server/key; note 'first server clockwise owns it'. Arc directions correct.

[start] ring with 5 keys + servers A/B/C, new server S3(emerald)@210 added; emerald arc 150->210 shows the stolen span; k2@200 recolored emerald (moved from S2 to S3); notes 'only keys in new arc move' + 'every other key stays' + '≈1/N of keys move'. Only one key moves — correct.

[mid] ring with 5 keys + servers A/B/C, new server S3(emerald)@210 added; emerald arc 150->210 shows the stolen span; k2@200 recolored emerald (moved from S2 to S3); notes 'only keys in new arc move' + 'every other key stays' + '≈1/N of keys move'. Only one key moves — correct.

[end] ring with 5 keys + servers A/B/C, new server S3(emerald)@210 added; emerald arc 150->210 shows the stolen span; k2@200 recolored emerald (moved from S2 to S3); notes 'only keys in new arc move' + 'every other key stays' + '≈1/N of keys move'. Only one key moves — correct.

[start] two rings: LEFT one-point-each (3 clustered servers, big rose arc = lumpy uneven ownership) vs RIGHT three-replicas-each (9 interleaved points, even small arcs = smooth); emerald arrow left->right; note 'each server hashes to many ring points so arcs even out'. Balancing pedagogy correct.

[mid] two rings: LEFT one-point-each (3 clustered servers, big rose arc = lumpy uneven ownership) vs RIGHT three-replicas-each (9 interleaved points, even small arcs = smooth); emerald arrow left->right; note 'each server hashes to many ring points so arcs even out'. Balancing pedagogy correct.

[end] two rings: LEFT one-point-each (3 clustered servers, big rose arc = lumpy uneven ownership) vs RIGHT three-replicas-each (9 interleaved points, even small arcs = smooth); emerald arrow left->right; note 'each server hashes to many ring points so arcs even out'. Balancing pedagogy correct.

[start] sorted server positions row [30,95,150,210,270,335]; key at 120 -> next >= 120 binary-searches to 150 (emerald highlight); 'owner=server @150'; O(log n) per lookup bound with legend 'n = number of ring points'. Binary-search-next-clockwise correct.

[mid] sorted server positions row [30,95,150,210,270,335]; key at 120 -> next >= 120 binary-searches to 150 (emerald highlight); 'owner=server @150'; O(log n) per lookup bound with legend 'n = number of ring points'. Binary-search-next-clockwise correct.

[end] sorted server positions row [30,95,150,210,270,335]; key at 120 -> next >= 120 binary-searches to 150 (emerald highlight); 'owner=server @150'; O(log n) per lookup bound with legend 'n = number of ring points'. Binary-search-next-clockwise correct.

[start] remove server S1@150 (marked ✗, dimmed); k1@80's owner spills clockwise past the gap to S2@270 (amber arc, k1 recolored); note 'only removed server's arc moves, every other key untouched'; ≈O(K/N) keys remapped bound. Spill-to-neighbour correct.

[mid] remove server S1@150 (marked ✗, dimmed); k1@80's owner spills clockwise past the gap to S2@270 (amber arc, k1 recolored); note 'only removed server's arc moves, every other key untouched'; ≈O(K/N) keys remapped bound. Spill-to-neighbour correct.
